title: Falls Prevention for Skilled Nursing Facilities

seo.title: Falls Prevention for Skilled Nursing | Able Care
seo.description: Protect Five-Star ratings. Defend VBP performance. Reduce rehospitalization. Objective falls screening for skilled nursing facilities in under five minutes.

blocks.backgroundImageAlt: Therapist conducting bedside assessment with patient
blocks.headline: Falls prevention for skilled nursing.
blocks.subtitle: Protect Five-Star ratings. Defend VBP performance. Reduce rehospitalization. Objective, standardized falls screening in under five minutes, usable by any member of staff.

blocks.heading: Four metrics that protect your ratings

metrics.name: Grip strength
metrics.description: Upper-body strength proxy. Low grip strength predicts falls, rehospitalization and functional decline in SNF residents.

metrics.name: Sit-to-stand
metrics.description: Lower-limb power and balance. A key predictor of discharge readiness and safe transfer ability.

metrics.name: Gait speed
metrics.description: The 'sixth vital sign.' Linked to rehospitalization risk, length of stay and VBP outcomes.

metrics.name: Timed Up and Go
metrics.description: Falls risk assessment in under a minute. Validated in skilled nursing and rehabilitation settings.

blocks.heading: How it works in skilled nursing

steps.title: Admit screening
steps.description: Run a five-minute Able Assess screening on admission. Establish baseline functional status objectively.

steps.title: Risk stratification
steps.description: Automatic risk banding against normative data. High-risk residents are flagged for intervention immediately.

steps.title: Ongoing monitoring
steps.description: Re-screen at defined intervals. Longitudinal trends surface decline before it becomes a fall or a rehospitalization.

steps.title: Outcome reporting
steps.description: Objective data feeds Five-Star, VBP and regulatory reporting. Defensible to surveyors, payers and families.

testimonials.quote: We caught decline six weeks earlier than we would have with our old process.
testimonials.attribution: Clinical Director, US skilled nursing facility

testimonials.quote: The objective data gave us something defensible to show surveyors. That alone was worth the investment.
testimonials.attribution: Director of Nursing, multi-site SNF operator

blocks.heading: Common questions from skilled nursing operators

items.question: How does Able Assess help protect Five-Star ratings?
items.answer: Falls outcomes feed quality measures that directly impact Five-Star ratings. Objective screening identifies at-risk residents earlier, enabling timely intervention before falls occur and protecting your quality scores.

items.question: Can CNAs run the screening?
items.answer: Yes. Able Assess was built for non-clinical staff. The workflow guides them through every step. No certification or training course required.

items.question: How does it integrate with our existing EHR?
items.answer: Able Assess is integration-ready with major EHR platforms. The clinical team can walk you through the setup during a demo.

items.question: What about residents who cannot stand?
items.answer: Grip strength can be assessed seated or supine. The workflow adapts to the resident's ability level while still providing objective, comparable data.

items.question: How quickly can we deploy across multiple facilities?
items.answer: Most multi-site operators deploy in under 30 days. One standardized protocol means minimal training overhead at each site.

items.question: What does it cost?
items.answer: Pricing is based on facility count and assessment volume. The ROI calculator models payback against one reduced fall per facility per month. Book a demo to discuss your specific scenario.

blocks.heading: See Able Assess in your skilled nursing workflow.
blocks.body: 20 minutes with a clinician who has deployed it in SNF settings.
